input:
rating 4.0. Curly Hair These flexi-rods are essentially the same as those that you would purchase at your local beauty supply store. The thing I like about the Ohuhu pack is that you have seven varying sizes and colors. There are six of each size. It also comes with the storage bag, as shown, which is great for storage and if you need to travel with them. They run about $13, as of the date of this review. The price isn't bad at all.

These are great if you want spiral curls or to add curls to the ends of your hair. If you are looking to do a full-body style, start at the back of your neck (nape) and use the smaller rods, working your way up to the bigger rods when you get to the middle and top of your hair.

They are super easy to use and because they are soft, it is not too difficult to sleep in.

Of note, I was sent these flex-rods directly, at a discounted rate, in exchange for my honest opinion.

rating 4.0. Don't be so quick to give up! My first time buying foam rollers was from Walmart, a pack of 18 conair rollers that came in 2 sizes for $15.. I was pretty disappointed when I saw this pack of 42 for $13 on amazon a few weeks after.

First, the sturdiness of these rods differ from the Conair ones I had purchased. They felt less firm, but found as you go down in size that changes. This can affect how well it stays in your hair, but I just use an elastic on the bottom part to keep my ends rolled.
Second, I have long hair so I found these to be a good length. Also, I combine the purple and blues together when needed, or the yellow and oranges (not very far in sizes and in case 6 isn't enough). I haven't tried the smaller sizes, but that's because I like bigger waves/curls.

I'm not much of a girly girl, so I found these perfect to do my hair the night before. Plus, no heat damage!! In the morning, I take them out, give my hair a few tosses and run my fingers through the curls and done. It may look a little silly at first, but give the hair a few moments to relax.

**Sleeping in them: I found the blues and purples to be the most comfortable, probably because they have the most cushion. Try not to roll to close to the scalp, some hairs might get rolled too tightly and are a pain in the ass when trying to sleep.

- For the girls not having success, you have to use it on damp hair! I've been rolling them in dry hair and then using a spray bottle to wet my hair then wrap them up in a scarf or shirt. I sleep in them and remove in the morning.

- I don't use hair spray, but the curls can last from one morning to the next! Of course, not perfectly but I like the way they look. I'm not a perfectionist at all, so I like the "effortless" beachy waves look.

rating 5.0. It's not super comfortable to sleep with them in my hair but ... I was looking up hair ideas online and I saw a video where these rollers were used. I tried it and it really was as simple as it looked in the video. It's not super comfortable to sleep with them in my hair but it is worth it to wake up with curly hair.

rating 5.0. Never burning my hair again. These work perfectly. I have waist length hair and I still have plenty of length to the rod left over. I don't use hair product when using these so they look a bit more natural. I can have them in for about an hour and I'm good to go. Of course the amount of time you keep them in affects the curls so that's all up to you. They're very flexible so you can tie them in just about any way. I love using the smaller rods for tighter curls.
rating 5.0. Worth it! These are great. There are many in the pack and they hold their shape well (creating nice curls). Nice gift for anyone with long hair too.

rating 5.0. y way i love these rods you get different sizes so you can curl hair any way you choose will purchase again love the price very good product.

rating 4.0. Easy to use for the clueless! I just ordered these and tried for the first time. I am absolutely CLUELESS about all things beauty, so did these on a whim last night and woke up with some great curls! I keep getting compliments at work too.
Sleeping in them wasn't super comfortable, but I'm surE I'll get used to it. Thankfully they're soft, so it's not terrible. They didn't stay in very well, but again, still woke up with great curls.

rating 5.0. These Fun Rods Will Deliver Curls I've used these flexible curling rods several times now. First, I practiced wrapping my hair up a few times at home without waiting the required time to get the curls. (I was so excited to try it out when they arrived.) I also tried it with both wet and dry hair. The hair wrapping goes pretty quickly because there are no fasteners or clips to worry about. That was a key factor in my decision to try these. You just roll up the hair from the bottom up and then bend the long ends up over the curl to secure. And they do stay put, once you figure out which way to bend them. Wrap tightly and bend the tips over the hair closest to the scalp. There are a few videos on YouTube that can help show you how to wrap your hair using curling rods of any kind, if you want to see it in action.

The rods are long and have a nice, slightly grippy texture on the surface, which is good for gripping the hair a little. But it's not too grippy that it would prevent easy removal from the curled hair.

They have a bit of cushioning to them, too, which means there's a little bit of give to them when you curl. The cushioning is also handy if you sleep in them. In general, it's not great sleeping with stuff stuck to your head. I managed to avoid getting a headache when I slept in these, though, which surprised me.

The ends are covered with little caps that keep the wire inside, and they kind of pop out of the rods a little, but not off. You can easily pop them back into place without any problems.

There are 6 of each size/color in the pack (7 sizes!) and you can choose a differing variety every time you set your hair, for differing looks. I have shoulder length hair and liked the smaller ones for the shortest hair at the nape of the neck, and graduate to larger sizes toward the top of my head. But I have used larger ones all around as well. It depends what kinds of curls you're going for. You can also use larger or smaller sections of hair to wrap each rod, for more variations.

The design of the clear/blue carrying case was also a factor in my decision. I like the upright design, the little zipper cap, and the handle on top. It's compact for the quantity of rods it contains, which makes it easy to travel with, though the vertical design can be difficult for getting all the rods back in and fitting well. It helps to straighten out the rods before putting them back in the container. Not a big deal, just a small struggle if you're short on time.

I have gotten crazy curly results from these after sleeping in these the other night. I was so curly I didn't recognize myself in the mirror! These rods will deliver! The key is to wrap moistened, but not sopping wet hair, wrap tightly and consistently. Mind those pesky ends and make sure they are held under the hair in the right direction, and leave these rods in for several hours until the hair is all completely dry. Overnight is best, but I've seen results in just a couple hours with only slightly dampened hair.

I'm still learning how to best curl using these, but I'm very happy with my investment. I think you will enjoy playing with your hair using these.

rating 5.0. Pretty cool They worked for my hair! I guess depending on how tight or which way you roll them depends on the type of curl you'll get. I'm definitely going to use them on my daughters hair.

rating 5.0. Great Product for Varying Styles I have tried many types of hair rollers over the years and these are definitely one of the best I have used. They are easy to use and not uncomfortable, even for sleeping, as many types are. These twist-flex rods also make it simple to achieve different type curls and work for all lengths and types of hair. I have used them myself and for my granddaughter to style our hair and we have both been pleased with the results. It is nice to find a product that works this well. Thank you.

rating 5.0. They Do Exactly What They Say They Will Do They do exactly what they say they will do. One of the white ends have broke off already, but its not essential to the product working. I have used them in my daughter's hair and they left her hair really curly. I would buy them again.

rating 1.0. Poor quality, don't waste your money. I bought these twist-flex rods based on the high sales and reviews. But honestly, these are poor quality twist-flex rods... They do not hold in your hair. It is a complete waste of your money. Don't buy them.

rating 3.0. Nice, but cheaply made. These are ok, just ok. These pictures show being in my daughters hair for only 15 minutes. I didn't want super curly but just a beach wave. Without the heat especially since she's little. She loves styling her hair different so I thought these would be perfect for her. It would have gotten 5 stars but the white plastic caps came off the ends of some when arrived and also when using and the metal is coming out... the white end caps are only tied on with a string. Also, the hairs do get caught on the white plastic part. It catches the hair under it and pulls which she kept saying hurt her as it yanked out her hair. I understand it hurts but it was so annoying having to be cautious. If it wasnt cheaply made then it probably wouldn't cause this to happen.

But other than that, they are nice however just cheaply made. I can't see these lasting very long and would probably purchase a different brand next time to see if I can find find one that's better quality.

Over all, I will continue to use them until all of them are falling apart, which probably won't be too long but yes they do work.

Also, to get this look on her: I first sectioned then sprayed with de-tangler to dampen the hair slightly. I then gave a really quick spray of hairspray to the mid-section to ends. Avoid the roots as you don't want it to hold there or be stiff (It would then section the curls and will be harder to have them all blend in together) I then rolled her hair into it rolling it. Waited 10 minutes, then 5 minutes blew dry it on high with the cold air. Un reveled it softly, don't pull!!

rating 5.0. So far great! My hair is already curly so I cant ... So far great!
My hair is already curly so I cant really say how it will work with someone with straight hair, but for me it keeps my curls in tact. That is pretty much what I wanted them. Like any curlers, the longer you leave them the more defined they'll be.

rating 5.0. I love them. I got a curl setting product to work with the twist flex rods and now get pretty curls that relax during the day. I put the curler by my scalp and wrap the hair around it then finish by putting the hair ends toward my scalp and turn the curler with hair closer to my scalp, made a loop of the twist flex (one half over the other) then bent that loop forward over the curls then brought the twist ends around the hair & loop to make a tight knot by my scalp. Try to get them toward the top of my head in front & sides so I can sleep without laying on a curler. I like the red in front, pink in the top back, and green ones I use along my neck line. My hair is still damp in places after 7 hours (I sleep in front of a box fan) so I just take them out and don't do anything with the curls until they dry completely then pull on the curls some. The curls last all day. I wash my hair every night but I have a feeling the curls could last longer.

Update: I now use no setting product just the rods. I use the green ones all over my head. I put the hair around the twist flex same way as before wrapping the hair around the whole twist flex rod then bend it in half putting the one half under the other, twist the ends together. So much easier and next day my hair is so loose and bouncy. I learned on tv yesterday to leave about 3 inches loose for the crimped style or double crimped style for a more relax style, so won't worry so much if the ends aren't twisted in.

rating 5.0. Different yes, I use it daily :) With cosmetics online it's difficult if you haven't seen the product first hand,choosing colors.

I swore I ordered a different color set than I received on this order. I haven't seen this Trio in store before.
The colors that I received, are a buff ivory (semi sheer) a taupe center (that I love) than is the bronzer which has orange undertones.

At first I cringed at the orange of the bronzer. Yet once applied they all blend well. It's a Fall / Winter combo for me.
I am enjoying the buff and taupe, very different from any blush, contour, this is new tones/colors compared to what I've used in the past.

rating 4.0. Works on pale skin, sorta! I have to start by saying that I am a super pale girl! Therefore, my review might not be the best frame of reference (unless you are also a super pale person). The contour and bronzer shade are amazing! The contour specifically is the kind of shade that is neutral enough that it doesn't look out odd. It functions really well. A light hand is always a good idea. It is easy to build, as is the bronzer! This actually might be one of my most favorite bronzers. I 10/10 prefer matte over shimmer. I mean, its more orange on my skin than it would be on more fair than light skin, but its the kind of shade that can dual as a blush too. I like it, it brings the life back in me. The only thing is the highlight shade. I cannot use it for my face. It makes a decent light color for the eyes if I need it. Its just too dark on me and cannot do the job. Still, I don't regret buying this. The packaging is plastic, but really nice! It comes with a brush, but I used my own. Contour and bronzer blend out really easily too! Lastly, theres a little mirror which is always a bonus!

rating 4.0. Not ideal for very pale skin, but usable For the record, I am a longtime Physicians Formula wearer, and their products can be hit or miss. I am not being paid to leave a review, or representing anyone but myself here. This is the first time I tried PF's countouring powder, and I've used it for about three seasons. I like it. I don't love it.

I have very pale skin with pink undertones that is uber-sensitive and I have to be very careful about sun exposure. Anything can make me break out in hives or eczema, at any time, even if I used it daily for years. Thankfully, PF products have never done that to me, in almost a decade.

As for this product, the shades are where I take some issue. They worked best for my tone in summer, but now, in winter -- I'm shopping for a different product.

* The dark shade is really mahogany-like, so I have to blend, blend, blend in daylight or risk looking like I smeared mud on my face. It's good for shading jawline and cheekbones with a light touch at temples if you need it. Beware, though. If you are very pale, you want a very light touch with this one.

*The medium shade is very subtle and helps blend and soften the dark. It also works well as a matte bronzer, around sides of the nose, and wherever you need just a little definition. This is the best shade of the bunch, honestly. At least for my skin tone.

* The light shade is, frankly, worthless to me. It gives my skin a pasty, chalky look and looks heavy. Yuck. I occasionally use it in tiny spots as a concealer, but as a contour powder, it just looks like I put on way too much setting powder or something. Instead, I use my lightest shade of matte eyeshadow (also a PF product) and a higher-end illuminator for highlighting. Those results are beautiful, frankly, even on my 38 year old skin.

All in all, PF's contour colors are suitable for pale skin, but may be better on other slightly darker or warmer skin tones, or on summer tanned skin.

rating 5.0. This is my favorite sculpting product The matte sculpting trio is my daily go-to product. (75ml) in one container.  This, of course, contains magnesium which also helps sleeping, and the manufacturer states clearly that each full pumpful delivers 25mg of elemental magnesium along with 3mg of melatonin.  I appreciate their specifying exactly what you are getting.  Since each pumpful delivers 1 ml of product, this container should deliver 75 dosages of product.  Unfortunately, MELATONIN SLEEP CREAM does not specify the amount of active ingredient you are getting, which I failed to mention in my review of it, although the jar contains 4 oz. of product.  So, there is no way to know how much melatonin you are getting since the product is not metered and the label doesn't tell you the melatonin content.  Both are good products, but I cannot compare dosages or contents of product delivered when you apply them.  The only reason I didn't give it five stars is because of the expensiveness of the product.

rating 5.0. Great for larger nail plate damage! This was my first time using silk wraps and I was using them for a slightly more complicated purpose; instead of just dealing with a crack in the nail, I have a full portion of my nail plate that broke off due to damage at my nail matrix. I wanted an easy way to keep my nails clean, dry and safe during the healing and regrowth process and decided to go with these. This is literally just a sheet of pure silk, and while I read that the tabbed ones are easier to use because they're already shaped and self-adhesive, I really wanted to have more control over it given my situation. This works extremely well with glue, and in addition to that, once filed down to be completely even, it's like a freshly buffed nail - however, in texture only. It does not necessarily look natural on its own. It provided me with a really solid foundation, and I was able to cover it with gel polish afterward. Now, the place where my nail bed was previously exposed is completely protected as well as feeling just as hard as my actual nail. It looks completely natural and normal and it makes me feel a lot better about having such a big part of my nail bed missing.

rating 5.0. Great nail repair I have used this product in the past and it is the best way I know to mend a broken nail.  The long strip of silk (as opposed to the product) makes this much more economical as you cut only what you need.  Just cut you little piece of silk, hold it on your nail and drop a tiny bit off crazy glue on.  I sometimes hold down edges with a little clamp, if necessary.  You can then smoothe the edges with an every board and polish as usual.  It actually could stay on for a couple of weeks, thru hand washing, housecleaning, gardening.  It never feels like the nail had ever been broken.  And it breaths so your nail underneath doesn't get dried out.

rating 4.0. Good power, would recommend but a few small changes would make the Micro-Pedi even better So far, I'm pretty pleased with this buffer, but there are a few small things that I think Emjoi could improve on. I was able to use this buffer for both feet after only charging for 30 minutes, then plugged it in for the recommended time. It would be nice if the red light ever went off to let you know when it was charged, but this hasn't been an issue since it's always charged when I need it. I don't know exactly how long a full charge lasts, because I've never run it all the way down buffing both feet (about 15 min). The power is good, although I could see that a man with really thick calluses might need more power. It does slow down a little if you press down really hard, but at least on my feet, pressing that hard hurts. So for my purposes, the power is just perfect. The blue buffer has lasted 4 uses so far and seems to still work fine, which is pretty good considering how much has buffed off my feet during these first uses. The primary reason why I'm giving 4 instead of 5 stars is because of two things: 1) the rotating head isn't viewable 360 degrees  (i.e. covered on one side), so it really limits the ability to see what you're buffing. It's particularly hard to use with your non-dominant hand on your opposite foot, because you can't see what's being buffed unless you're looking at the skin from the side. I have friends who have the Amope, which allows you to see your foot better, but has insufficient power and isn't rechargeable. 2) the buffer/roller on this Micro-Pedi is inset pretty deeply between the side pieces meaning that you can't really buff anything that's not convex  (i.e. can't really buff flat or concave surfaces on your feet, only things that stick up/out like heels and toes). This means that I can't adequately buff the callus just below my 2nd toe on the ball of my foot, which is a slightly concave area. As this is my worst callus, that was very disappointing. Press as hard as I might at different angles, I just can't get that spot with this buffer. But for my heels, toes, and the rest of my foot, it's great. So overall, I would recommend this product based on the batteries saved by recharging, the power, the comfort in your hand, and it's effectiveness on most parts of my feet. But I wish the side pieces didn't stick so far out beyond the roller limiting usefulness on concave parts of the foot, and I wish the roller wasn't covered on the back limiting visibility.

rating 5.0. Micro-Pedi did not work after 2nd charge but replaced Received the product, charged it up per directions, it worked fine. 2013-08-25 This is an UPDATE!!!!!! Micro-Pedi did call back within 48 hours. I explained what occurred to a skeptical but businesslike person who was persuaded that the product was defective and sent me another one. The new one works great. rating 5.0. Emjoi Micro-Pedi Pro Foot Buffer Rechargeable. I love this product. It performs exactly as advertised. I have used it 4 times so far without needing to recharge. These uses were not just for a moment or two either, but for a considerable time. There are many complaints of dust everywhere. If held properly, with the handle downward, and a trash can under your fee, the movement of air from the rotation of the buffer will push the dust out vents on the back of the micro-pedi into the trash can or other receptacle. If you hold it upside down... you are going to get dust because that air flow will blow it into the room. There is less dust from this than the traditional foot file I have used for years. The rotation of the buffer is such that "scrubbing" of the machine is wasting time. Moving the Micro-Pedi in a downward motion is how you will get the greatest buffing action and greatest results in the least amount of time.

I am finally able to get the callouses on my toes under control and the snaggy bits on my heels gone in one sitting. A few minutes a couple of times a week keeps me from sticking to the carpet. I wish I had this years ago. I was a manicurist for years. This really is a great tool.

I have received no compensation for this review. I have purchased this product for my own personal use only.

rating 5.0. I highly recommend.... I highly recommend the rechargeable Micro-Pedi. I bought my first one about 4 years ago and love it. I use it about every other day and it has dramatically improved my dry heels and calluses. Recently I noticed it wasn't working as well as it used to and the battery wasn't holding a charge as long as it used to. Then it occurred to me that I had been using it so long, the rechargeable battery was just wearing out. But I think for the price, 4 years is a pretty long time for something I use consistently, and the motor is still working just fine. Perhaps a replaceable battery will be in future versions, but I was happy to purchase my second Micro-Pedi.

I use genuine Emjoi refills and they last me at least a couple of months. I also discovered Gold Bond Rough and Bumpy cream which I use after the Micro-Pedi every other day. The combination has made my feet look much better. Helpful hint: always use the Micro-Pedi on dry skin that is free of lotion for the best results.

rating 3.0. Fine for sanding light calluses. Poor for removing heavy calluses If you have calluses that are a few millimeters in thickness that just need to be made smooth, the Emjoi may be the perfect solution for you. However, if you have serious calluses that actually need removal, rather than just minor shaping, you will find the Emjoi to be very, very slow, about 10 times slower than the product I eventually replaced it with, the Microplane foot rasp.

The Emjoi works by literally sanding the skin, something that requires the skin be dry at the time, and therein lies the problem. The Microplanes, which work by slicing away a tiny layer at a time, work on skin damp from bath or shower, and that skin is much easier to remove than hard, dry skin. Removing my thickest callus on my male feet took close to 20 minutes with the Emjoi. The Microplane eliminates it in fewer than two minutes. The Microplanes are also a lot cheaper: The one I use, the Microplane Colossal Pedicure Rasp, is currently listed for $9.50 on amazon. The more feminine versions start at $8.95.

No, I have no connection with the Microplane company. I just found it shocking when, after spending a whole bunch of money on the Emjoi, I discovered an alternate technology that was so much better. I just wish I'd found out sooner. Do make sure you have dampened your feet before using the Microplane. Otherwise, much of the advantage disappears. (Don't dampen your feet before using the Emjoi: it has no way to clear any dampened removed skin, so it just fills the nooks and crannies in the sandpaper until the cylinder is perfectly slick.) If you search amazon for "microplane" and "foot," you'll find the full line.

rating 5.0. A GREAT Product! My thanks to the other reviewers and the Q's & A's for helping me decide to 'splurge' on this product! I was looking for quality nail care products so that I can do my own mani/pedis when I prefer to. I decided on the "Pro" version, as I don't like the expense and hassle of frequent battery replacement/disposal. I wanted something I could use outside, to eliminate added indoor. This definitely generates a lot of dust particles, because it works so well! I'm sure the dust factor is considerably less once your feet are in better shape. For me, using it the first time, there was a lot! But it was GREAT, as I could see that it was being effective, even though it felt gentle. In the hopes of helping others evaluate whether or not to purchase this, I'll add some tips based on my experience:

- Be sure to follow the instructions and charge it for 16 hours before using it the first time. The battery is very important, and you would not want to compromise its performance.
- I used it the first time with the blue roller that came with the unit. It took me quite a long time - about 45 minutes, to make substantial progress on both of my heals, before the battery started losing power. I re-charged it and used it again the next day. Had I known how this works, I would have purchased the more abrasive green roller to use first. I've now ordered two of those - one for me; one for my husband, as I am going to have him use this (he doesn't know it yet). I like the fact that the rollers come in plastic containers, so I can keep his separate from mine.
- I've also ordered the mani/pedi attachments so that I can use them for nail care/prep before polishing my nails. I hope they work well.
- The unit is compact - easy to store.
- It is easy to clean. It doesn't appear that the 'dust' gets into any moving parts, so hopefully, it will be easy to maintain/will last a long time.

rating 4.0. Corded vs. Cordless Review We wore out our battery operated Emjoi after 3 years. So we bought this one with built-in lithium ion batteries that are rechargeable and I read you can use it with the cord plugged it even if the batteries are dead. We learned 2 important things. NO, You cannot use it at all if batteries are dead and you plug it into the wall socket, at least ours doesn't. Secondly, you cannot take out the batteries and put in fresh ones. You must plug into the wall socket and wait a couple of hours. The average run time on the fully charged Lithium Ion is 5 minutes and then it is dead as a paperweight. Our previous battery only operated model would run for about 30 ~ 45 minutes before needing either new batteries or we used rechargeable AA batteries. So I am confused as to why the rechargeable Lithium Ion's don't last near as long compared to normal rechargeable batteries. The seller is sending me a new one and I'll try it out over the weekend to see if it performs better. As far as foot sanders go, Emjoi is the best brand & quality I've found.

rating 1.0. Disappointing I have thin nails. It's hard to grow them out because they'll bend and then split at the sides. So when I came across a product that would act as a thickener instead of just a strengthener, I was excited to try it.
I followed the instructions and applied two coats on clean nails. My nails were still bendable and within a couple hours, it started flaking off. So I decided to put on a thicker third coat. It still flaked off hours later and my nails still bent. I think I get more structure from regular clear nail polish. So disappointed with the results of this product.

The picture is after that and a full day. It is still very nice!
I have an autoimmune disease as well as a deficiency in potassium, magnesium, and calcium. I take a large amount of medication that has damaged my hair, skin, and nails. I highly recommend Nail-Aid Ceramide Extreme Thickener!

current_product_reviews: rating 3.0. No huge impact :| The cost was reasonable, and it arrived quickly and was easy to apply. However, I was bummed to see no noticeable impact on the strength or thickness of my nails after using this. I'm an anxious nail bite-er, and sometimes I can go quite awhile without any issue but then BAM! all my nails are gone and my nail beds are bleeding. Its embarrassing, and I generally will cover it with acrylic nails while they heal and grow out. However, my nail shop is not in business yet, so I thought to try this. When my nails are healthy, I have strong, clear, straight nails (I don't have damage due to acrylics); so I had a bit of expectation that I would get closer to that with this product. Nope. Not much different from your average top coat.
other_product_reviews: rating 3.0. Nice variety - poor handles. I like the variety of this set, but I was frustrated that the coating on the handles began to corrode almost immediately that any acetone touched it. I could expect this from makeup brushes, because you don't use acetone with makeup, but you commonly use acetone with nail art - so I was surprised.

It does make your hair shiny and smooth. If you are looking for a small hairbrush that is not a wire brush, this is a good one. I would recommend buying this brush.

This type of brush is very versatile, and would also be perfect for brushing a child's hair, or even a small animal! I really like the solid wood handle on the comb; it's well made and seems quite durable.

rating 5.0. Great Portable Beard Brush I bought this brush for my beard. I wanted to experiment with a boars hair brush vs Kent comb and wood comb. In short, I'd say if you're looking for a boars hair brush, this is a good one. I would definitely describe it as a portable medium stiffness brush. Boars hair brushes are great for helping orient hairs in specific directions when you're either growing out or styling your beard. The first time I used this on my cheek line, I was surprised by how well the skin beneath was exfoliated. Really enjoy this, even though I don't use it for the main body of my beard.

input: rating 1.0. I'd used RapidLash prior to this and it really grew ... I used RapidLash prior to this and it really grew my lashes long. I tried Uptown to see if it would also improve the thickness of my lashes. I didn't see any growth at all after using it for over 2 months. Actually, I've lost all of the longer lashes I'd gotten from RapidLash, which has changed it's ingredients after being sued by Allergen who makes Latisse. I've used several other highly recommended growth serums but none work. BECAUSE there are no ingredients that will promote growth. There are some with impressive peptides but that is not for growth and the serums don't actually penetrate the lashes. It looks as if Latisse is the only real deal. Expensive, but the real deal.

rating 1.0. Don't waste you $ I had used 2 more expensive products and had grown very long lashes. I purchased this hoping to at least maintain my lashes for a month or so and save some $$. I won't use it again and am glad I didn't buy for my friends.

rating 5.0. 2/16/16 - This is truly amazing stuff! 2/16/16 - Eyelash Growth Serum for Long Eyelashes From Uptown Cosmeceuticals

This is truly amazing stuff!

First, let me say, no one has bribed me, coerced me, or given me freebies or a discount for my review. This is my honest experience since I received this product in January 2016.

I have always had short eyelashes. When I've used an eyelash curler in the past, my eyelashes looked nice to those standing directly in front of me, but from the side, my lashes looked unnaturally and abruptly turned upwards. I'm Japanese and have a very slim, below-the-crease eyelid, which means the upper eyelid area is substantially larger. With age, my upper eyelid has gotten heavier and a bit droopy, smothering my eyelashes. I always figured, "that's life."

Well, years ago my niece was going to try an eyelash growth serum, but was told she needed to see her eye doctor before they would give it to her. When she saw her eye doctor, she discovered that she had an eye problem that prevented her from being permitted to use the eyelash growth serum (not this particular eyelash growth serum. I think it must have been a prescription, back then. That's why she had to see her eye doctor before they'd give it to her.) Her eye medication had the same ingredient as the eyelash growth serum, so she could not use both. Several months ago, she told me about this and she said that her medication actually made her eyelashes grow.

A few weeks back, I was searching Amazon for mascara and I ran across these eyelash growth serums. I had no idea what the "active ingredient" was that made them work, so I had no idea what to buy, especially since there were so many to choose from. I suppose I could have done an "eeny, meeny, miney, moe", but I read the reviews on a few. There were a couple where the serum was to be applied directly to the lashes. Perhaps, they work; I don't know. But, what I do know is the hair, itself, is dead; its growth occurs within the hair follicle beneath the surface of the skin. Perhaps, the eyelash-applied serums are designed to slide back down the lashes to reach the skin and then seep into the hair follicle. It makes sense until you think about applying mascara--it would cover-up the serum. If the ultimate goal is for the serum reach the skin to bathe the hair follicle, why not apply it directly to the skin? So, this helped me with my selection.

When I reviewed this particular eyelash growth serum, most of the reviews were pretty good. The company, Uptown Cosmeceuticals made some fairly solid declarations: up to 25% thickness and length after two weeks, and 75% increase after six weeks of regular use. Of course, "up to" does not exclude 1% thickness & length, but the 25% & 75% measures were an impressive possibility. The clincher was the company's guarantee: if you're not completely satisfied, they'll refund your purchase. Another thing: all of the pictures--many reviewers provided pictures of the progress of their eyelash growth--a week, two weeks, three weeks, etc. Plus, so many reviewers stated that they saw increased lash length within a few days. I figured, what the heck; I've wasted more than $20 on useless stuff. I might as well try it.

I wish I had taken pictures, too. I know this sounds absurd, but I'd swear that my eyelashes grew after the first application ... I mean, a noticeable length. After four days, I could see them just peaking out from my upper eyelid. I received the serum on Jan 27th. Today is Feb 16th. My eyelashes are about 1/8" above my upper eyelid--no exaggeration. Now, when I use my eyelash curler, instead of a single abrupt bend in my eyelashes, I have enough lash to place several soft bends to give my lashes a natural curl. I'm truly amazed! My eyelashes were always so short. Now, I literally have something to flutter. There's something about eyelashes that makes a woman feel feminine.

After two weeks, when I knew there was growth, that it wasn't my imagination, I started applying the serum to my eyebrows. I have a relatively large, bald scar where I was hit on my brow-bone with a rock, and the outer edges of my eyebrows thinned out after I went through "the change." I was curious to see if it would help. It's only been a week, but there's definitely an improvement. It's not clear to me if there is new hair growth, but my existing brow hairs are definitely longer. The extra length covers the scar in my left brow so well, that a couple of times, I turned to my right brow to look for the scar--it wasn't as blatantly obvious as it has been for the last 40 years!

There is one recommendation I'd make to anyone who tries this serum:
After two weeks, I noticed that my lashes on the left seemed to be slighly longer than those on my right. I think this was because I was applying the serum to my left lash-line, first. Without re-dipping the brush into the serum, I'd apply the serum to my right lash-line. I think my right lashes weren't getting as much of the serum, as the left. So I started applying the serum to the right lash-line, first. Once my lashes appear equal in length, I'll alternate which lash-line I apply the serum first, or re-dip the brush to give each lash-line a full brush of the serum. One last idea: another reviewer said that once her lashes reached the length she desired, she reduced her application of the serum to once a week. I thought that was a good idea.

To anyone who purchases this serum because of my contribution to its accolodes, I hope it works as well for you as it has for me.

rating 3.0. Not my favorite lash serum This is the second brand of eyelash growth serum I've tried, and decided to try this brand because it was considerably cheaper. This is another example of "you get what you pay for".
In the past I have used Rapidlash. I was amazed at how long and full my lashes were after only a few weeks. I'd say it took about 3 weeks for it to really show, and the results were amazing. Over time I got a little lazy and took my new full, long lashes for granted and stopped using Rapidlash. Gradually my lashes returned to their normal short, thin condition.
I knew I needed to start using lash growth serum again and decided to try Uptown Eyelash Growth Serum. It was cheaper, and advertised longer, thicker lashes in just two weeks.
It's been about 3 weeks ... and, yes, my lashes are somewhat thicker and longer. If I hadn't tried Rapidlash in the past, I would think lash serum a waste of money. Not really enough difference to justify the price or inconvenience. What this experience did show me is that there are great lash serums out there, this just doesn't appear to be one of them.

current_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. This just might be the lash serum of my dreams. I just got this a couple of weeks ago and didn't start using it right away. I'd gotten out of the routine of using lash serum, so I had to remind myself to use it. Well, now that I've been using it consistently, I can honestly say it's at least as good as the high priced serums I'd used in the past (Revitalash and Latisse). My lashes are already starting to show the results. It's a record. With prior products, I had to wait much longer to see visible growth. I can actually feel them touching my eyelids when I blink.

As an aesthetician, I make recommendations on lash products to my clients (especially those who get lash extensions, whether they suffer from traction alopecia or not). I'm not going to have any problem recommending this product. The ingredients are top notch.

It was slightly darker than the 1B's I'm used to but it wasn't jet black like the #1 color so it still worked for me. The cut is very nice and neat. All I had to do was straighten it and cut the lace and it's good to go. When straightening it, there was a a lot of shedding and little fibers coming off but after combing it once done, the shedding stopped. I only had one issue with this wig that I've never had with a wig before. The cap didn't seem stretchy enough which caused the wig to slide back a bit quite often so I have to readjust a lot. Perhaps the cap is too small for me (but I've never had that issue before with any wig). I don't really mind though because I needed this wig for a party and not for everyday (my natural hair is a lot longer than 12").

input: rating 4.0. Pretty good so far I've never really used a "base coat" before, but I heard they help! So I ordered this and received it quick! I went to go do my nails and put on one coat and it dried so fast! No waiting around long to start my colored coat of nail polish. Now lets hope it truly does help keep your polish on ;)

rating 1.0. Not impressed I was very excited to recieve this base coat.  I am attempting to grow my nails longer, now that I am getting older.  I would never purchase this product agaian. This base coat does not keep my polish on, and it creates not barrier for my darker polished. I put this on and then painted two top coats and my nails were still stained red from the polish.  I am very dissatisfied.

rating 5.0. very effective! This stuff is pretty amazing! I've just recently discovered Seche Vite topcoat, which is a flipping MIRACLE product - but once the top of my polish became basically bullet-proof it became very clear that my only obstacle to a really long-lasting manicure was getting the damn stuff to actually stick to my natural nails. I asked the googlemachine for suggestions, and taDAH! this popped up.

Although I typically wear pale sheer pink on my hands so that wear and chips are less obvious, I put this to the test with a cute new hot pink polish... and I kept it on for 10 days! That is unheard of for my nails. Truly mind-blowing.

Good stuff! A very inexpensive and easy way to extend the wear of your nail polish.
rating 5.0. I love it!!! Its awesome, Its holds for a while, and It even looks good by itself! lol Has a very shiny coating to it and its soo cute!

rating 5.0. China Glaze brand is always good I have never been disappointed in the china glaze brand. This adhesion base coat really makes the polish stick, and doesn't take too long to dry, even though I use it with color and then a top coat.

rating 4.0. Okay! Makes my nail polish stick better and the drying time is fast. I do 1 coat of this, 2 coats of my polish, 1 more coat of this and then finish it off with China Glaze's Good to Go topcoat. It last for 7-9 days!

rating 2.0. I don't see how it is better than no base coat For me, the color nail polish coat adheres to this fine (why wouldn't it?), but within a few days the base coat separates from the nail. I'm not sure what the advantage is versus using no base coat at all.

Update June 2014: It might not be the product's fault. I learned that some people have naturally oily nails, just like hair and skin can be oily, and this would inhibit good adhesion. I'm supposed to de-grease my nails with alcohol first, and then base coats should adhere better. When I get around to trying that I'll post another update with my results.

rating 2.0. Did not work for my thin nails I have yet to find a nail polish or nail polish / base coat / top coat combination that actually works on my nails. This base coat did not extend the life of my manicure any more than polish alone. Perhaps it's a coincidence, but my nails also took longer to recover after I removed this product. They usually get flaky at the tips after I wear nail polish, but after using this base coat, my nails continued to flake for over a month. I only wore it once.

the blush does not go on very choppy, but it isn't as smooth as it could be. a bit of blending is necessary, but not enough to be a pain.
before purchasing, i would have liked to know that the palette is split in to 2 smaller palettes. while it isn't a big deal, it bothered me that in the photo they were trying to make it look like it was one large palette.
this palette set is DEFINITELY worth the price. both are small enough to fit in a purse, and contain a small thin mirror for touch ups.
upon arrival, the package was not damaged in any way. it even came 2 days before i expected! needless to say, i was pleased.

input:
rating 4.0. Endless combinations The colors turn out darker than the picture shows. I experimented with other gel shades and came up with some brighter options.
Must use gel polish and "no wipe gel topcoat".
Use a tiny amt. Of powder over the top coat. Rub in. Dust off remaining loose powder and add 2nd topcoat.
Powder flies everywhere so cover your workstation for easy cleanup. Wear a smock!
*photo #1show all 12 powders over black.
*photo #2 shows 4 of the powders over black. The samples opposite of them are the same powders done with other gel colors.
Experiment with them to find shades you like.

rating 4.0. Not bad There is definitely a learning curve with this product as with any powder chrome effect nail decoration. You definitely need a tack free/no wipe gel top coat. The colors are beautiful however they aren't full on true chrome. I buffed and buffed my nails to get the perfect chrome look however once I put the top coat on to seal in the design, small microfine particles would pull up causing the design to have a chrome effect with microfine glitter floating above. It looks pretty don't get me wrong however it isn't a true chrome. I recommend this product regardless because if you decide to get the full 12 color package the possibilities are endless  and it's great to practice with.

rating 5.0. Ladies this is an update after reading other reviews, if you are get glitter look top coat not cured enough, try that! I was so excited to get this new glitter powder! Came in 7 days. I have put on color, taken it off numerous times to see what works the best. I am a pro at it now!
Here are step by step that you HAVE TO DO to get it to turn out. I am working on getting coats thinner. I know they will look even better after that.
1. Base Coat and UV Light
2. I used Black and two of the samples are grey. One THIN coat and UV Light.
3. Second THIN black coat and UV Light.
4. No wipe top coat. This is a must!!! One thin coat and UV Light.
5. Pick a color of the Glitter Powder. All they have is numbers on the small containers so I did practice ones on some fingernail rings I had. My nails pictured are silver at base and blue at top. Pictured in the shade when you see grass. Pictures in the sun when you see concrete.
6. None wipe top coat and UV Light.
Tricks: a makeup brush works good to get color on nail. I found if I dipped a finger lightly in powdered and put it on with my finger the color got more true to color.
The picture of the two grey ones are first one base coat black, second used grey base coat.
These are not in numerical order:
005 Silver
002 Light Blue
011 Dark Blue
009 Purple
008 Teal
005 Dark Purple
012 Rose Gold
006 Pink
003 Green
007 Goldish Green
004 Royal Blue
001 Dark Green
These were all with just one coat of nail glitter. If you repeat glitter and top coat again will be more vibrant. My nails you see have been on my hands for 4 days! I have washed floors, did cleaning and still nice enough to go to a wedding! I have NEVER had so many compliments on my nails.
I can't stress enough thin, thin, thin coats. Like I said I need to work on that myself!
Would I buy it again? A big YES!!!
Prior to buying this product I tried eye shadow, nope. I tried 100% pigment, nope. Has to be made especially for nails like this product.
** I did not get paid or free products for my review. I did it because I wanted to. Had a lot of fun with it but in beginning can be frustrating if steps are not done correctly. My nails are my own. I had two people ask me where I get my manicure done!!!
Have fun with it...best nail product to come around in years.

rating 5.0. Great product for fun nails I'm happy with this product but you have to do it correctly. I recommend going to YouTube and looking at some tutorials on using mirror/chrome products. The key is using a no wipe clear coat over the color, or black polish for silver chrome, using just a bit of powder and rub a lot with the sponge wands provided. You really don't need much! I was also very happy to see the order show up much earlier than expected!

rating 5.0. Mirror mirror on my nails I love this product so much! Once you catch the hang of the steps to take to get the best results you will be in love also. For the best results these are the steps that I took:
1. Shape nails and clean throughly, DO NOT APPLY OILS OR LOTIONS
2. Apply gel base and cure for 30 secs
3. Apply gel color and cure for 30-60 secs
4. Apply thin coat of no wipe gel top coat and cure for 30 secs
5. Apply powder using eye shadow brush and rub in completely over entire nail...apply a little pressure to make sure powder is sticking
6. Brush off any excess powder before applying a clear top coat and cure for 30-60 secs
7. After making sure that the nails are completely cured wipe off using a no lint cotton pad and alcohol

rating 3.0. Buy another set that doesn't have translucent powders THESE ARE NOT ALL COLORED. That being said, when you see the powders yourself, you'll think you got 8 of the same white powder. The white ones are color shifting powders, but are pretty crappy without color to them.

I spent about an hour prepping sample sticks on black polish, and I labeled them with sharpie. As you can see, because the white powders are not actually chrome, they look terrible in direct sunlight. The light completely washes out any tint the powder might have. Only in indirect light do they look nice (except #1, #1 looked awful).

I'd probably buy another set that had color shifting chrome powders that weren't translucent.

rating 5.0. Very Nice! I've never used gel polish before, but got some so I could try to get a mirror effect manicure - something I always dreamed of having but was not possible until this kind of powder product. Results were gorgeous, just what I wanted, real mirror effect with the silver powder over black. I used my fingers to apply the powder, and it worked best if rubbed a lot with very light pressure. Harder pressure made my nail look a bit lumpy from pushing the briefly cured gel around, but I still need to practice with gel in general. Two coats of powder looked about the same as one (perfect!) and it wasn't terribly messy to clean up. I used a peel off base coat because the thought of removing gel horrified me and I like to change polish colors pretty often. It worked just fine: the polish was smooth and stayed on but I was able to get under it and pop the color off like a little shiny shell when I wanted it gone :) I am ready to try the other colors, and over different bases, so fun!

Honestly, I am posting this review because I'm just so happy to finally have the manicure of my dreams, yes, but more because the packaging was really thoughtful and nice. Everything is pretty, there's a nice storage box with a clear soft plastic cover, reference card with instructions, applicators and a nail wheel for practicing/testing. The powders each have a foil seal to protect the product during shipping. This was a great value and a super nice product.

input:
rating 5.0. Beautiful Wig! (especially for the price) This wig is DEF a buy! It's a light Swiss lace with a large amount of parting space on the crown.
Several inches of ventilation on the sides with varied tying to create a more natural hairline.
Pre-cut baby hairs at the nape of the neck!
Super soft! Doesn't seem to tangle easily. It's very easy to comb my hands through.
The color is a beautiful ashy blonde. A cool, 613 color, with nice dimension to it.
Overall, a definite BUY! Great for Daenerys, a light blonde Elsa, Aurora, Cinderella, or every day wear!

rating 5.0. WOW [[VIDEOID:104bd4b0c214449de22e3843906e08db]] Wow is all i can say! When you see wigs on here for such a good deal it sometimes seems too good to be true. I am completely in love with this wig! I am attaching a picture and a short video. The picture is after I cut the lace, put on a wig cap to cover my half black hair, used a small amount of hair glue and then blended in with my foundation. The video is before I finsihed cutting the lace and did all of the above. First, I am a HUGE wig lover. I have a little over 40 now from so many different brands. This wig has fastly become a favorite. The quality is just crazy good for what you're paying. The hairline is beautiful and so easy to make look real.  But also I LOVE that it IS a free part and can be parted any way. I highly recommend it! You cannot beat the price. I am shocked with just how much I love it.

rating 5.0. Great bang for your buck; beautiful blonde shade. I will update after a few more wears, but so far, so good.
I cut it, did a curl set and steamed the curls with a clothing steamer to set and brushed out.
The shade of blonde is very pretty, it is sort of a lightest ash blonde, not entirely platinum but not as yellow as a #613.
It has dimension and just the right amount of shine.
Super soft.
It will obviously tangle, being so long and synthetic, but so far I wore it for a day and night and it brushed out just fine. The ear flaps go down too far on the sides for me, but I have a tiny head.
Good value for money!

rating 3.0. Damaged? Prepurchased? Or just made from bad hair? I believe this wig would have been pretty damn good if it hadn’t been worn before me or some how damaged before getting to me. I don’t know what the case is I just know there were two spots inside the cap where the clips to adjust the cap are that were visibly dirty. And once the wig was on the entire top back half of the wig is all broken little hairs that stick straight up making the wig look tore up for being brand new. Also as soft as the wig was it had that worn in feeling when it comes to a synthetic wigs that are starting to get a rough after being worn a few times or not being cared for the best. There were itty bitty tiny knots throughout the wig especially in the back. I literally took the wig out, carefully, looked at quickly since I was in a major hurry. From what I initially saw I LOVE it. It is beautiful the lace blends in beautifully with my skin. I wish the roots were shaded a little but it is still beautiful... EXCEPT for the fact the entire top back is all little broken hairs sticking straight up and the wig feels like it’s ragged after an hour of wearing it. Which both of those reasons make it not worth a penny.  I honestly don’t know if it’s an amazing wig that I just got extremely unlucky with and got one that was pre purchased and the seller didn’t notice or care they had damaged it or it was not taken care of before it came to me and somehow was used because those tiny tight knots don’t appear out of nowhere. I would have thrown it in the bag and sent it right back for a new one but I was desperate for a wig that day so was stuck with what I got.

rating 5.0. Hands down the best wig ever!!! I have been buying wigs for about 7 years now and I can honestly say this is the best quality wig I have gotten yet. This is the second time I have purchased this wig and definitely will be purchasing again. The hair is so soft, the hairline is realistic and doesn’t  shed or tangle. Every other wig I have purchased I have had to add hair to,  fix the hairline and cut. I didn’t have to do anything to this wig... it’s PERFECT!!  And the price can’t be beat. I have spent twice as much on other wigs that didn’t even come close to the quality of this one. If I could give it 10 stars I would! And to top it off it comes with eyelashes and a wig cap too!! rating 1.0. I feel duped! I am a female suffering with embarrassing hair loss and this was my first wig. I bought it for myself after reading extensive reviews and looking for weeks I chose this wig.
I regret it and here's why:
-You can't style it or part it realistically.
-Even when left down you still end up seeing the tracks by everyday movement.
-It tangled instantly & requires constant brushing.
-its too tight and uncomfortable for 23" head

Even after I chopped it to my liking (for snapchat filter pictures) I knew I would never feel comfortable actually wearing it out in public.

And at first I thought this was normal and I thought I would have to spend hundreds on a decent wig. But a few days ago my friend got me a wig that I absolutely adore and its not only from Amazon but its cheaper....so what the french toast is up with all the positive reviews on this poorly made over priced wig??

rating 1.0. The Wig You Receive is NOT the Wig in the Pictures I had never returned an Amazon product before, but what I received was so ridiculous that I decided it had to be returned. The wig that you receive is nothing at all like the product photographs. I don't know how Amazon has not demanded this seller upload more truthful imagery, especially considering the price. The quality is like a cheap Halloween costume wig you'd buy at a Dollar General for a few bucks. The hair strands resemble fishing twine. My mother saw the hair, and it was the first comment out of her mouth. They look shiny and obviously plastic. The wig began to shed immediately upon removal from the package, something I have never experienced before with my wigs. The wig isn't even styled the same as the product photographs. Instead of the loose beach waves presented in the product photographs, the product I received had extremely tight curls, so tight that the length was reduced significantly, meaning the tips of the wig barely came to my shoulders. The wig itself was quite thin and very tight (my head is the average width, yet both the wig and its accompanying cap were very painfully snug on me, failing to cover my hairline). The color was more of an old grey than blonde, and there is NO PART IN THE WIG. I know that the product photographs demonstrate a central part, but there is no part, and it is IMPOSSIBLE to part the hair yourself in a restyle because of the way that the wig is made. Anyone with a shred of common sense will know that you're wearing a wig because of the super weird, partless, and unnatural hairline. The thinness of the wig I previously mentioned is appalling. It isn't beautiful and realistically full or youthful. You will look like a balding Lucius Malfoy from "Harry Potter." I was too embarrassed to take pictures of myself in the wig. I decided to return it within ten minutes. The only good things I can say about this wig are that it came in a cute little bag with free fake eyelashes and that the return process was fortunately very smooth. I actually ordered another wig from a different Amazon seller at the same time for only about $20, and it was 1000000x better than what I received from this seller. Please beware! What is shown in these pictures is NOT what you receive and is definitely not worth the very high price. You can get a much better wig for around $20. I honestly don't know how this wig has received any good reviews, unless the seller switched manufacturers for this product at some point and now is delivering a much poorer quality item.

input:
rating 5.0. Beautiful Wig! (especially for the price) This wig is DEF a buy! It's a light Swiss lace with a large amount of parting space on the crown.
Several inches of ventilation on the sides with varied tying to create a more natural hairline.
Pre-cut baby hairs at the nape of the neck!
Super soft! Doesn't seem to tangle easily. It's very easy to comb my hands through.
The color is a beautiful ashy blonde. A cool, 613 color, with nice dimension to it.
Overall, a definite BUY! Great for Daenerys, a light blonde Elsa, Aurora, Cinderella, or every day wear!
rating 5.0. Very pretty! Minimal shedding! This blonde color is super platinum and beautiful, thick and full with minimal shedding, glad I purchased!

rating 5.0. WOW [[VIDEOID:104bd4b0c214449de22e3843906e08db]] Wow is all i can say! When you see wigs on here for such a good deal it sometimes seems too good to be true. I am completely in love with this wig! I am attaching a picture and a short video. The picture is after I cut the lace, put on a wig cap to cover my half black hair, used a small amount of hair glue and then blended in with my foundation. The video is before I finsihed cutting the lace and did all of the above. First, I am a HUGE wig lover. I have a little over 40 now from so many different brands. This wig has fastly become a favorite. The quality is just crazy good for what you're paying. The hairline is beautiful and so easy to make look real.  But also I LOVE that it IS a free part and can be parted any way. I highly recommend it! You cannot beat the price. I am shocked with just how much I love it.

rating 5.0. Great quality for the price I wore it for Halloween as the Mother of Dragons and it was perfect! The color and curl pattern were just right. The hair feels nice compared to those cheap costume stores.
rating 5.0. Amazing Great quality for such cheap price ! I love it
rating 5.0. Great wig Looks so real. I love it

rating 5.0. Great bang for your buck; beautiful blonde shade. I will update after a few more wears, but so far, so good.
I cut it, did a curl set and steamed the curls with a clothing steamer to set and brushed out.
The shade of blonde is very pretty, it is sort of a lightest ash blonde, not entirely platinum but not as yellow as a #613.
It has dimension and just the right amount of shine.
Super soft.
It will obviously tangle, being so long and synthetic, but so far I wore it for a day and night and it brushed out just fine. The ear flaps go down too far on the sides for me, but I have a tiny head.
Good value for money!

rating 5.0. Decent Wig for it’s price This was my first time ever using a front lace wig, I did have a little trouble getting the wig to sit against my forehead smoothly without glue while also trying to make it look natural. It does tangle a little bit and sheds a bit aswell while trying to style it, but for $35 this is a pretty decent wig! If you’re constantly using wigs and know what youre doing I’d say this would probably look amazing. It is a little heavy but not to where it’s bothersome. I posted some photos with this so you get an idea, the first photo you can obviously see I tried to pull some baby hairs out to make it blend better but it didn’t really work too well haha

rating 5.0. Cosplayer Approved! I live for this wig- I wear it all the time for fun, and I have several cosplay plans involving it. I can wear it for long periods of time, and it fits and sticks like a charm with just a little bit of spirit gum. It’s a bit blonder than it looks in the pictures, but it still works perfectly.
rating 1.0. Do not buy! If i could rate it zero stars i would! It was choppy and uneven not one length and the tracks were showing. And the lace was dirty looking. It was disgusting!

rating 3.0. Damaged? Prepurchased? Or just made from bad hair? I believe this wig would have been pretty damn good if it hadn’t been worn before me or some how damaged before getting to me. I don’t know what the case is I just know there were two spots inside the cap where the clips to adjust the cap are that were visibly dirty. And once the wig was on the entire top back half of the wig is all broken little hairs that stick straight up making the wig look tore up for being brand new. Also as soft as the wig was it had that worn in feeling when it comes to a synthetic wigs that are starting to get a rough after being worn a few times or not being cared for the best. There were itty bitty tiny knots throughout the wig especially in the back. I literally took the wig out, carefully, looked at quickly since I was in a major hurry. From what I initially saw I LOVE it. It is beautiful the lace blends in beautifully with my skin. I wish the roots were shaded a little but it is still beautiful... EXCEPT for the fact the entire top back is all little broken hairs sticking straight up and the wig feels like it’s ragged after an hour of wearing it. Which both of those reasons make it not worth a penny.  I honestly don’t know if it’s an amazing wig that I just got extremely unlucky with and got one that was pre purchased and the seller didn’t notice or care they had damaged it or it was not taken care of before it came to me and somehow was used because those tiny tight knots don’t appear out of nowhere. I would have thrown it in the bag and sent it right back for a new one but I was desperate for a wig that day so was stuck with what I got.

rating 5.0. Hands down the best wig ever!!! I have been buying wigs for about 7 years now and I can honestly say this is the best quality wig I have gotten yet. This is the second time I have purchased this wig and definitely will be purchasing again. The hair is so soft, the hairline is realistic and doesn’t  shed or tangle. Every other wig I have purchased I have had to add hair to,  fix the hairline and cut. I didn’t have to do anything to this wig... it’s PERFECT!!  And the price can’t be beat. I have spent twice as much on other wigs that didn’t even come close to the quality of this one. If I could give it 10 stars I would! And to top it off it comes with eyelashes and a wig cap too!! I am in LOVE!!!! Looking forward to purchasing other products from this seller. I’m very impressed!!

rating 1.0. I feel duped! I am a female suffering with embarrassing hair loss and this was my first wig. I bought it for myself after reading extensive reviews and looking for weeks I chose this wig.
I regret it and here's why:
-You can't style it or part it realistically.
-Even when left down you still end up seeing the tracks by everyday movement.
-It tangled instantly & requires constant brushing.
-its too tight and uncomfortable for 23" head

Even after I chopped it to my liking (for snapchat filter pictures) I knew I would never feel comfortable actually wearing it out in public.

And at first I thought this was normal and I thought I would have to spend hundreds on a decent wig. But a few days ago my friend got me a wig that I absolutely adore and its not only from Amazon but its cheaper....so what the french toast is up with all the positive reviews on this poorly made over priced wig??
rating 1.0. DO NOT BUY FOR THIS AMOUNT Ran fingers through once and it was shedding and tangled but described as resilient!
rating 1.0. Not worth it It's very bulky and heavy.  Nothing at all like the description said. It sheds so much hair and it does not cover all my hair.

rating 1.0. The Wig You Receive is NOT the Wig in the Pictures I had never returned an Amazon product before, but what I received was so ridiculous that I decided it had to be returned. The wig that you receive is nothing at all like the product photographs. I don't know how Amazon has not demanded this seller upload more truthful imagery, especially considering the price. The quality is like a cheap Halloween costume wig you'd buy at a Dollar General for a few bucks. The hair strands resemble fishing twine. My mother saw the hair, and it was the first comment out of her mouth. They look shiny and obviously plastic. The wig began to shed immediately upon removal from the package, something I have never experienced before with my wigs. The wig isn't even styled the same as the product photographs. Instead of the loose beach waves presented in the product photographs, the product I received had extremely tight curls, so tight that the length was reduced significantly, meaning the tips of the wig barely came to my shoulders. The wig itself was quite thin and very tight (my head is the average width, yet both the wig and its accompanying cap were very painfully snug on me, failing to cover my hairline). The color was more of an old grey than blonde, and there is NO PART IN THE WIG. I know that the product photographs demonstrate a central part, but there is no part, and it is IMPOSSIBLE to part the hair yourself in a restyle because of the way that the wig is made. Anyone with a shred of common sense will know that you're wearing a wig because of the super weird, partless, and unnatural hairline. The thinness of the wig I previously mentioned is appalling. It isn't beautiful and realistically full or youthful. You will look like a balding Lucius Malfoy from "Harry Potter." I was too embarrassed to take pictures of myself in the wig. I decided to return it within ten minutes. The only good things I can say about this wig are that it came in a cute little bag with free fake eyelashes and that the return process was fortunately very smooth. I actually ordered another wig from a different Amazon seller at the same time for only about $20, and it was 1000000x better than what I received from this seller. Please beware! What is shown in these pictures is NOT what you receive and is definitely not worth the very high price. You can get a much better wig for around $20. I honestly don't know how this wig has received any good reviews, unless the seller switched manufacturers for this product at some point and now is delivering a much poorer quality item.

rating 3.0. No parting space The wig is very soft, looks beautiful,but there is absolutely no parting space. Like absolutely none. You can only wear it like in one style that will hide the tracks. That cute little braid up style looks a mess in real life because all everyone can see is the weave tracks. Near the weave tracks the hair is not so silky. The wig would be perfect if it had some parting space and more lace in the front.

rating 5.0. Home electrolysis Excellent item--this one actually does what it says.  Must follow directions carefully or you can create scarring.  This is my second of this type of unit.  The other one lasted ten years.  Laser hair removal is great for many areas, but it has limitations.  This fills that gap!
rating 3.0. It works but you need good light a magnifing glass and lots of time and start in a place that you don't care about mild scarring because it takes some time to get the hang of it.

rating 2.0. Not for me Used it a couple times (or attempted anyway) and maybe its just a little too complex for me, but I wasn't getting any results except for a few uncomfortable red spots. I'm intrigued by the technology but I wouldn't recommend it.

rating 4.0. Great Price for this technology This is a great value for an honest electrolysis machine.  There is a bit of a learning curve to using it for sure, and electrolysis is certainly a slow painstaking process to begin with.  The instructions are a bit lacking but an understanding of what you are trying to accomplish helps.  I found it to work best if I would grip the stylus in-front of the conductor band with my thumb and middle finger, dip my index finger in the saltwater solution, slide the needle into the targeted hair follicle and then after seating the needle I would put my moistened index finger on the conductor band to complete the circuit and start the electrolysis process.  The beep of the machine is a bit loud but tolerable and it is nice that it pauses about every 5 seconds so that you can better gauge contact time.  I have zapped about 50 hairs over the past couple months and so far they seem to be staying gone.

rating 5.0. It really works and very affordable. Just takes time. I purchased this electrolysis machine because it works. I had the same one over 15 years ago. I used it on my legs and face. Where I used it the hair has never grown back. I got rid of it because it took a long time to treat each hair and opted for Laser Hair removal. After only a few years the hair grew back where I had the Lightsheer laser hair removal done. Nothing has grown back where I had done the home elecrolysis with this machine.

The product has been modified slightly since the one I had over 15 years ago, but the instructions are identical. The instructions actually show the old model in the pictures. Follow the instructions and it will work.

I was really excited that I could purchase the same product that I once owned and am confident that it will work because it did before.

rating 2.0. Ouch! Ok, I had used one of these in the past and thought I'd give it another try - I'm so tired of plucking chin hairs daily. You do need to keep your fingers wet with salt water - to get a good charge. My problem was that I had to repeat a couple times for the hair to loosen up enough to come out. The next day, my face was covered with raw open sores in the spots I treated. I had the sores for over a week, and then pock mark scars! I won't be using it again. To be fair, maybe my skin is more sensitive now - I don't remember this problem when I used it before. But just beware.

rating 3.0. works but after you die this product really works but not on all areas, it works good for me on my lower arm, prepare your self befor using it because this device is super tedious device, so it's not for large areas or thin hair size, it do hurt a bit and it has an annoying sound and bad tweezer.

rating 5.0. Be prepared for what you buy I have bought 3 of these over the years and I can attest they work as a charm. I have permanently gotten rid of my middle chest hairs and unwanted hairs in other places. It is also relatively unexpensive. The drawbacks are pain(no problem for me but if you are too sensitive consider using a xylocaine gel) and very very time consuming to do large areas. A trick I have found that helps lessen time needed and pain and scarring is take some instant glue and tip the tip of the stillet inside. After it dries use some tweezers to remove the glue from the very tip of the stillet. This way the currect will go only to the root and not the rest of the follicle.
rating 2.0. Sizzle lean This was a lot harder then what i expected to use so I cant really blame the product .. I feel its more of the way I used it.

rating 1.0. Disappointed The stylet tips break way to easily. Just to use the tweezers to straighten out the needle when it bends the thing broke on me twice in less than one week!!!!!To cheaply constructed!!!!!

rating 4.0. Does work but has a learning curve and takes patients. At first I was feeling like it was impossible to use this device.I was determined to give it the best chance I could.I will have a few scars from the figuring it out process but after I got the hang of it I saw that it actually does work. Its tedious, and slow and does hurt a little bit in certain areas like under the nose and near the lips. I have learned a few tricks that make it easier to use. A lighted magnifying mirror is a must. Reading glasses help too. Ont thing that made it much more usable for me was I out tape over the metal band on the stylus and then I can out it in the follicle without it being energized. When I get it where I want it I touch the metal plate on the base unit with my left hand and it works fine. I will say this isn't a toy and can burn your skin if your careless.The only complaint I have at this point is its hard to treat very fine hairs as the stylus is too large to fit into very small pores.If anyone wants advice or more info Ill be glad to help. I had been getting electrolysis treatments for a while now and its extremely painful and expensive. At least this gives me the chance to do some of it on my own. Anyone that says this device is worthless or didn't work just didn't take the time to figure it out.
rating 1.0. GARBAGE I WISH I WOULD HAVE LISTEN TO MY GUT, BUT I DIDN'T I'M A VICTIM OF THIS CRAP. PLEASE PLEASE DO YOURSELF A FAVOR, AND SEE A PROFESSIONAL!!. NOT WORTH THE MONEY,TIME & ENERGY!.
rating 1.0. hair remoal Good looking product, however very poor quality, it removes the hair but takes on everage 2 hours to remove a single hair, so its impossible to work with very time consuming.

rating 4.0. Good product I have a problem with black course hairs growing on my chin, which often grow under my skin. I would have to use the tweezers every day and still ended up with scars. This product was very tedious to use, but worked well. Its been a week and no hair has returned in the areas where the hair was treated correctly. As a result, my skin has been able to heal without interruption from hair regrowth and for the first time in a long time my skin color is returning. If you have time and patience I recommend this product for small areas. I rated 4 instead of 5 due to time and lack of ease for use. The item was received in a short period of time as described. I would order again from Larkin Supply.
